evals.name: full-pipeline-planning
evals.prompt: I want to create a CLI for Google NotebookLM (https://notebooklm.google.com/). I need to be able to create notebooks, add sources (PDFs, URLs, text), ask questions against my sources, and manage audio overviews. Plan out how you'd approach this using the /cli-anything-web pipeline.
evals.expected_output: Should reference the 8-phase pipeline from HARNESS.md, mention Chrome DevTools MCP for traffic capture, discuss auth (Google OAuth), identify key API endpoints to capture (notebooks CRUD, sources CRUD, query/chat), and outline the CLI command structure.

assertions.text: Mentions Chrome DevTools MCP for traffic capture

evals.name: test-planning-for-notebooklm
evals.prompt: I've already built a basic cli-web-notebooklm CLI with commands for notebooks (list, create, delete) and sources (add-url, add-pdf, list). Now I need to write the test suite. Help me plan the tests — what should go in TEST.md Part 1, what unit tests do I need, what E2E scenarios should I cover?
evals.expected_output: Should produce a TEST.md Part 1 plan with test inventory, unit test plan per module (client.py, auth.py), E2E scenarios (fixture replay + live), realistic workflow scenarios (create notebook → add source → query → verify response), and subprocess test plan using _resolve_cli.

assertions.text: Produces TEST.md Part 1 structure (inventory, unit plan, E2E plan, scenarios)
Includes realistic workflow scenario (create notebook → add source → query)
States tests must FAIL (not skip) without auth

evals.name: standards-check-notebooklm
evals.prompt:
I just finished building cli-web-notebooklm. Before I ship it, can you check what I might be missing? Here's my current structure:

notebooklm/agent-harness/
├── NOTEBOOKLM.md
├── setup.py
└── cli_web/
    └── notebooklm/
        ├── __init__.py
        ├── notebooklm_cli.py
        ├── core/
        │   ├── client.py
        │   └── auth.py
        └── tests/
            └── test_core.py

What am I missing to pass validation?
evals.expected_output: Should identify missing files against the 50-check checklist: __main__.py, core/session.py, core/models.py, utils/ directory (repl_skin.py, output.py, config.py), commands/ directory, tests/TEST.md, tests/test_e2e.py, README.md. Should reference the 8-category structure and give actionable fix suggestions.

assertions.text: Identifies missing utils/ directory with repl_skin.py, output.py, config.py
Identifies missing README.md inside the package
Gives actionable suggestions (not just lists missing files)
